author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

The bug was as the diagnosis described:
`Storage::mutate_once` re-reads the record on a CAS-loss
retry but the verb-specific precondition check ran ONCE
before entering `mutate`, so the retry's closure blindly
re-applied the same mutation to a record that no longer
satisfied the precondition.

Fix shipped as Option B (tri-state closure return):
`MutateOutcome::{Write(ops), Skip, Conflict(err)}`. Closures
now inspect the freshly-read record and decide on each
attempt. `mutate` returns `Result<bool>` (true = commit
landed); `Storage::claim` exposes this via a new
`ClaimResult::{Claimed, AlreadyOurs}` so the CLI's `ready
--claim` path can distinguish "I claimed it" from "the
racer beat me and the same-user idempotent skip fired."
The CLI surfaces `AlreadyOurs` from a fresh `ready --claim`
as a typed `claim_race_lost` envelope (exit 1).

Migration sites with a moved-inside precondition:
`claim` (status + assignee), `unclaim` (status + idempotent
skip), `block` (status), `unblock` (status + idempotent
skip), `update` (slug uniqueness probe), `add_dep_edge`
(target-existence probe). Sites without domain preconditions
(`set_title`, `set_status`, `set_body`, `set_assignee`,
`add_label`, `remove_label`, `remove_dep_edge`) just got
their `Ok(vec![...])` rewrapped as `MutateOutcome::Write(_)`.

New storage-level tests
(`crates/jjf-storage/tests/integration.rs`): `claim_returns_claim_result_claimed_when_fresh`,
`claim_returns_already_ours_on_same_user_idempotent`,
`claim_on_closed_does_not_silently_reopen`,
`unclaim_on_closed_does_not_silently_reopen`,
`block_on_closed_does_not_silently_reopen`,
`claim_by_different_user_does_not_silently_overwrite` —
each pins both the typed surface AND the "no commit landed"
half of the contract.

# Recommendation

Close. `parallel_ready_claim_limit_one_assigns_two_different_ids`
passes 5/5 against V3 (all five runs deterministic PASS,
~1s each). V2 storage-level coverage rides on the same code
path; the new V2-storage tests exercising the moved-inside
preconditions all pass. V2 end-to-end repro at the CLI
level wasn't run (would need a v2-bookmark planter for the
`claim.rs` integration fixture, since `jjf init` now writes
v3 by default per ticket 3) — but the V2 mutate path
shares the closure dispatch, so the fix applies identically.
`cargo nextest run --workspace` green: 452/452 passed
(up from 446; +6 new tests).

# Confidence

High. The bug-class is narrow and the closure-as-precondition
contract is mechanical. The new `ClaimResult` return + CLI
`claim_race_lost` envelope are an addition, not a contract
change for existing callers (storage-level `claim` callers
that `unwrap()` still work — they just get a `ClaimResult`
back instead of `()`). One subtle point worth flagging for
tickets 4+: the `update` closure now does its slug-collision
probe on every retry against the post-race bookmark — a
single-slug-claim race with a `update --slug` will now
properly fire `slug_collision` if the racer landed first,
where before it might have silently rewritten the field.

# Open follow-ups

- `claim_race_lost` is a new CLI error kind; docs/cli-json.md
  should pick it up when the docs sweep happens. Not blocking
  for ticket 4+.
- V2-shape CLI fixture for `parallel_ready_claim_limit_one_assigns_two_different_ids`
  would let us run the 5/5 against V2 end-to-end too; rolling
  it into ticket 4's v2-to-v3 migrator sweep is the natural
  home.
- The `update` slug-collision probe-in-closure adds an extra
  `find_open_slug_collision` call per attempt (was: 1 probe
  pre-mutate; now: 1 per attempt — at most 2 with the 1-retry
  policy). For the live planner's ~10 issues this is
  negligible; if v3 issue corpora grow large enough that the
  slug probe becomes hot, an open-slug index (separate
  ticket) is the cleanup.

Branch: `v3-mutate-recheck` in `~/p/jjforge-bug-mutate`, commit
`19fb6237`. DO NOT push (orchestrator integrates).
